title: Differences between the production model and the test type
display_title: Differences between the production model and the test type
wikitext:
[]: Differences between the production model and the test type             Quote Post by Final Messenger » Wed Aug 24, 2011 7:39 am Can anyone tell me if they have seen any noticeable differences between eva unit 1 (test type) and the production models. I know that there is a difference between unit 0 and the other models because in episode ten it is said that it can’t be equipped with special equipment (in that case it was type D equipment). When I re-watched the series recently I tried to see differences between unit 1 and unit 2 and I only saw two small differences one their knives are different ( unit 1’s resembling a combat knife and unit 2’s resembling a box cutting knife) and the spike shooters that are in unit 2’s shoulder. But can’t unit 1 be equipped with a spike shooter (also I am pretty sure progressive knives are interchangeable). So that leaves the question are there any differences between the production model and the test type. By the way the harpies do not count (there are obvious differences here)

[]:             Quote Post by KnightmareX13 » Wed Aug 24, 2011 8:26 am The production models including unit 02 where derived from Adam's sample while Unit 01 was derived from Lilith.

[]:             Quote Post by palehorseXIII » Wed Aug 24, 2011 12:14 pm Unit 2 might be a little better fit for combat. It did take out thirteen MP-Eva's. (before they all regenerated of course.) I don't know might just be talking out my ass here but it seems a thought.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Aug 24, 2011 12:31 pm Prototype -> Test Type -> Production Model is supposed to represent a progression. I think it's like with Prototype -> Pre-production -> Production in cars. Eva-01 being Lilith-based instead of Adam-based kind of fucks around with this whole idea. One could make the case that they didn't even know Eva-01 had anything to do with Lilith when they started the show, but, even then, Eva-01 doesn't fit into a progression in other ways. For example, she has many armor parts that are unique to her, whereas Eva-02+ has armor more similar to Eva-00 (pre-upgrade). palehorseXIII wrote: (before they all regenerated of course.)

[]: Straight from the Horse's mouth. B)             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Wed Aug 24, 2011 12:38 pm Unit 2 might be a little better fit for combat.

[]:             Quote Post by Sephizim » Wed Aug 24, 2011 12:51 pm Also, the Test Type designation for Unit-01 could be related to the idea of testing what would happen if an EVA was made from Lilith. Unit-00 was the prototype, where they perfected the design of what an Evangelion actually was (the first success after the countless failed versions of Unit-00). Once they knew how to make an EVA that would (more or less) work, their next test was to see if they could replicate the same process from Lilith. Given the results of their data, all further models were made from Adam, as (presumably) this was the most effective way to manufacture an EVA. Unit-01 was designated as the first EVA because it was the first one they made after knowing (more or less), what they were doing, but Unit-02 was the first production model, because they made it knowing how they were going to go about making all the rest. Or at least, that's how I understand it. I don't know whether or not that really answers the question (or if that just rehashes what's already been said), but there you are.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Aug 24, 2011 2:28 pm palehorseXIII wrote: It did take out thirteen MP-Eva's.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Thu Aug 25, 2011 3:54 pm I always got the impression that the production model was mainly made for taking down angels, while EVA 01 was always intended for apocalyptic purposes...

[]:             Quote Post by Sephizim » Thu Aug 25, 2011 4:53 pm Kendrix wrote: I always got the impression that the production model was mainly made for taking down angels, while EVA 01 was always intended for apocalyptic purposes...

[]:             Quote Post by palehorseXIII » Fri Aug 26, 2011 1:08 pm NemZ wrote: She fought nine of them, not thirteen. 13 is just the highest number designation among them, but it starts counting at 05

[]:             Quote Post by Final Messenger » Fri Aug 26, 2011 1:18 pm Saw the teaser for Q. I guess we can add super rocket pack to the differences list (although unit 1 could be equipped with it I suppose. Guess we’ll have to wait for Q to see)

[]:             Quote Post by BornIn1142 » Fri Aug 26, 2011 1:21 pm Rebuild is a different continuity and not really relevant to the discussion.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Fri Aug 26, 2011 5:26 pm Final Messenger wrote: Saw the teaser for Q. I guess we can add super rocket pack to the differences list (although unit 1 could be equipped with it I suppose. Guess we’ll have to wait for Q to see)

[]:             Quote Post by The Abhorrent » Fri Aug 26, 2011 8:01 pm When you think about it, all of the Evangelions except the mass-production ones seen in EoE are more or less experimental... or at least custom-built. The differing origins of Eva-01 (made from Lilith rather than Adam) throws a wrench into the mix as well. Technically speaking, each stage along the development cycle should be more effective and powerful in combat than the previous; improvements in design and fabrication were implemented... though fiction is known for playing up the effectiveness of earlier models. This could largely be true for all the Evas except Eva-01 again, and any differences could be due the origins rather than the theory of narrative causality. Still, here's my brief analysis on the matter. Unit 00 -- Prototype The reason it's a prototype is simple, it was made as a "proof-of-concept". There were actually several prototypes (episode 23, particularly the director's cut), and Eva-00 was simply the first one which actually worked. Unit 01 -- Test Type Testing can mean many things; but assuming it doesn't refer the different origin of Eva-01, "stress-testing" and "experimentation" come to mind. Unlike the rest of the Evas, Eva-01 is meant to be pushed to its limits (stress-testing) and possibly exceeding whatever might be needed in the field.... which possibly includes going WAY over-budget. FOR SCIENCE! Unit 02 -- Production Model Eva-02 is likely just the first model which could be replicated in its design. If you wanted to build more than one, you could. Oddly enough, it ended up being one of the most unique Evas, being the only one with four eyes. Capabilities are probably the baseline for all future models, and potentially less than Eva-01 due to budget constraints not allowing it to have all the bells & whistles the test type gets. Units 03 & 04 -- Production Models The facial design is more reminiscient of Eva-01 (just missing the horn), but they're considered production models according to the wiki. Like one would also expect from a production model, they're palette-swaps of eachother (reportedly). Capabilities are probably similar to Eva-02, but Eva-04 did have an experimental S2 engine (or something along those lines); I can't recall if Eva-03 had it as well, but that brings us to... MP Evas -- Mass Production Models A working S2 engine which allows them to fight without a power cord and regenerate, which technically makes them the "best" of the Adam-derived Evas. Unfortunately, the Kaworu-derived Dummy Plugs are either incompetent at combat (meaning they couldn't legitimately beat Asuka until her Eva ran out of power) or sadomasochistic (meaning they let Asuka beat them because they like feeling & inflicting pain). Their cruel grins certainly imply the latter, but that's the subject of another debate. Anyhow, the MP models are better than the baseline production models (02-04) because it's an update on their design... though it could be only the S2 engine, wings, and the lances.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Aug 26, 2011 11:32 pm For reference, Unit 03 had no S2 Engine, so it's more like 02 than 04.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Fri Aug 26, 2011 11:53 pm AuraTwilight wrote: For reference, Unit 03 had no S2 Engine, so it's more like 02 than 04.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sat Aug 27, 2011 12:55 am Hyper Shinchan wrote: Also, FWIW Eva-04 has been reclassified as "Next Generation Test Model" rather than Production Model in NME, a logical choice since it wasn't a regular production model at all.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Sat Aug 27, 2011 1:23 am Bagheera wrote: But again, NME isn't relevant here.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sat Aug 27, 2011 1:29 am Hyper Shinchan wrote: I wonder if this is really a wise policy, there's some serious potential for useless threads' duplication.

[]:             Quote Post by arkiel » Sun Aug 28, 2011 7:14 pm We really don't know too much about how Eva are made. Unit One might have a unique construction, since we see what is implied to be her growing from Lilith in one of the screen-flashes. All Adam-based Eva could have been constructed in this manner prior to Gendo acquiring Adam. In fact, this would be a pretty good motivation for doing so. Forget Unit One, I'd be after someone that had control of both ways to manufacture Evangelions, too. Anyway, here's how to empirically measure the progression of development: Unit Zero - apparently can't be modified to take special equipment. Progenitor unknown. Unit One - implication is this was the test-bed for Production-Type tech, like the shoulder harness system. Unit Two - could be the first and only Eva to come equipped with that break-open control loop scheme. Equipped with shoulder harness, frangible blades, and sabot rounds. Unit Three - Four - Possibly includes some really convoluted yet hard scientific explanation for super stretchiness. Possibly they threw a Stretch Armstrong into the cloning process for fun. Unit Five - Can fucking fly. Armor design apparently easy to mass-produce, unless the "Mass Production Model" refers to the Units being vat-grown, without the need for a progenitor (in the proposed Unit One/Lilith sense).

Article: Differences between the production model and the test type
 00 -- Prototype The reason it's a prototype is simple, it was made as a "proof-of-concept". There were actually several prototypes (episode 23, particularly the director's cut), and Eva-00 was simply the first one which actually worked. Unit 01 -- Test Type Testing can mean many things; but assuming it doesn't refer the different origin of Eva-01, "stress-testing" and "experimentation" come to mind. Unlike the rest of the Evas, Eva-01 is meant to be pushed to its limits (stress-testing) and possibly exceeding whatever might be needed in the field.... which possibly includes going WAY over-budget. FOR SCIENCE! Unit 02 -- Production Model Eva-02 is likely just the first model which could be replicated in its design. If you wanted to build more than one, you could. Oddly enough, it ended up being one of the most unique Evas, being the only one with four eyes. Capabilities are probably the baseline for all future models, and potentially less than Eva-01 due to budget constraints not allowing it to have all the bells & whistles the test type gets. Units 03 & 04 -- Production Models The facial design is more reminiscient of Eva-01 (just missing the horn), but they're considered production models according to the wiki. Like one would also expect from a production model, they're palette-swaps of eachother (reportedly). Capabilities are probably similar to Eva-02, but Eva-04 did have an experimental S2 engine (or something along those lines); I can't recall if Eva-03 had it as well, but that brings us to... MP Evas -- Mass Production Models A working S2 engine which allows them to fight without a power cord and regenerate, which technically makes them the "best" of the Adam-derived Evas. Unfortunately, the Kaworu-derived Dummy Plugs are either incompetent at combat (meaning they couldn't legitimately beat Asuka until her Eva ran out of power) or sadomasochistic (meaning they let Asuka beat them because they like feeling & inflicting pain). Their cruel grins certainly imply the latter, but that's the subject of another debate. Anyhow, the MP models are better than the baseline production models (02-04) because it's an update on their design... though it could be only the S2 engine, wings, and the lances.
